title: Prepare for idler pulley assembly removal
summary: Safely prepare the tipper for idler pulley assembly removal by performing lockout/tagout, removing the station side panel if motor access is needed, positioning the A-axis and counterbalance between the threaded holes in the actuator base, verifying carriage alignment relative to the rail holes, and installing M6 socket-head screws below both carriages so the A-axis and counterbalance cannot drop when belt tension is released.
when_to_use: Use this procedure when preparing the OptiSweep tipper for idler pulley assembly replacement/removal and before belt tension is released in the idler pulley assembly service task.
not_for: Do not use this as a complete idler pulley assembly replacement procedure; the packet only supports the preparation and early removal setup steps.
Do not use if lockout/tagout cannot be completed.
Do not use if the A-axis and counterbalance cannot be positioned and secured with M6 socket-head screws as described.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Perform lockout/tagout, remove access panels if needed, position the A-axis and counterbalance, verify carriage alignment, and install the M6 socket-head screws to prevent dropping before belt tension is released.

safety_notes: Perform lockout/tagout on the tipper before beginning removal preparation.
Secure the A-axis and counterbalance with M6 socket-head screws before belt tension is released to prevent dropping.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the station side panel and motor area

access_required: Access to the station side panel and motor area
system_preconditions: Tipper is available for maintenance preparation.
A-axis and counterbalance can be manually positioned between the threaded holes in the actuator base.

visual_references.description: Maintenance figure for the idler pulley assembly referenced in the idler pulley assembly replacement procedure.

steps.title: Lock out the tipper
steps.purpose: Place the equipment in a safe maintenance state before accessing internal components or releasing belt tension.
steps.instruction: LOTO the tipper as referenced by "Operator Station Lockout/Tagout" on page 2.
steps.expected_result: The tipper is locked out and safe for maintenance access.
steps.healthy_condition: Lockout/tagout is completed and the tipper is in a maintenance-safe state.
steps.failure_condition: Lockout/tagout cannot be completed or the tipper is not in a safe maintenance state.

actions.label: Perform tipper lockout/tagout
actions.exact_action: LOTO the tipper as referenced by "Operator Station Lockout/Tagout" on page 2.

source_evidence.text: LOTO the tipper as referenced by "Operator Station Lockout/Tagout" on page 2.
The removal steps instruct technicians to LOTO the tipper.

steps.title: Remove the station side panel if motor access is needed
steps.purpose: Expose the motor access area when required for the idler pulley assembly removal preparation.
steps.instruction: If needed for motor access, use a 3/8-in. wrench or socket to remove the nine self-tapping fasteners securing the side panel, then remove the station side panel.
steps.expected_result: The station side panel is removed when needed and the motor access area is exposed.
steps.healthy_condition: Nine self-tapping fasteners are removed as needed and the side panel is removed without blocking access to the motor area.
steps.failure_condition: The side panel cannot be removed when needed or motor access remains obstructed.

actions.label: Remove side panel fasteners
actions.exact_action: Use a 3/8-in. wrench or socket to remove the nine self-tapping fasteners securing the side panel.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Idler pulley assembly maintenance figure for the service area associated with motor access and removal preparation.

stop_or_escalate_if: Motor access is needed but the station side panel cannot be removed.

source_evidence.text: If needed for motor access, use a 3/8-in. wrench or socket to remove the nine self-tapping fasteners securing the side panel, then remove the station side panel.
The removal steps instruct technicians to remove the station side panel if needed to access the motor.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Figure 7-21 appears on page 185 in section optisweep_operation_and_maintenance_manual_final_1_7_3_20_1_removal.

steps.title: Position the A-axis and counterbalance
steps.purpose: Move the A-axis and counterbalance into the required location so the retaining screws can be installed below the carriages.
steps.instruction: Manually position the A-axis and the counterbalance between the threaded holes in the actuator base.
steps.expected_result: The A-axis and counterbalance are positioned between the threaded holes in the actuator base.
steps.healthy_condition: Both assemblies are aligned in the actuator base so the adjacent rail holes can be used for securing the carriages.
steps.failure_condition: The A-axis and counterbalance cannot be positioned between the threaded holes in the actuator base.

actions.label: Position A-axis and counterbalance
actions.exact_action: Manually position the A-axis and the counterbalance between the threaded holes in the actuator base.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Idler pulley assembly area and surrounding service location used during A-axis and counterbalance positioning.

stop_or_escalate_if: The A-axis and counterbalance cannot be positioned as described.

source_evidence.text: Manually position the A-axis and the counterbalance between the threaded holes in the actuator base.

The removal steps instruct technicians to position the A-axis and counterbalance between threaded holes in the actuator base before inserting M6 socket-head screws to prevent dropping when belt tension is released.

steps.title: Verify carriage alignment with the rail holes
steps.purpose: Confirm the carriages are correctly located relative to the rail holes before installing the retaining screws.
steps.instruction: Verify there are two holes adjacent to each rail and that the carriages are positioned between the holes on the respective rails.
steps.expected_result: Each rail has two adjacent holes and each carriage is positioned between the holes on its respective rail.
steps.healthy_condition: The carriages are visibly centered between the adjacent holes on the rails, allowing the retaining screws to be installed below both carriages.
steps.failure_condition: The required adjacent holes are not identified or the carriages are not positioned between the holes on the rails.

actions.label: Inspect rail holes and carriage position
actions.exact_action: Verify there are two holes adjacent to each rail and that the carriages are positioned between the holes on the respective rails.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Service area around the idler pulley assembly and adjacent A-axis/counterbalance components to confirm carriage position relative to the rail holes.

stop_or_escalate_if: The carriages are not positioned between the holes on the respective rails.

source_evidence.text: Verify there are two holes adjacent to each rail and that the carriages are positioned between the holes on the respective rails.

steps.title: Install M6 socket-head screws below both carriages
steps.purpose: Mechanically secure the A-axis and counterbalance so they cannot drop when belt tension is released.
steps.instruction: Thread M6 socket-head screws into the holes below both carriages to prevent the A-axis and counterbalance from dropping once belt tension is released.
steps.expected_result: M6 socket-head screws are installed below both carriages and the A-axis and counterbalance are secured against dropping.
steps.healthy_condition: Both carriages are supported by installed M6 socket-head screws and the A-axis and counterbalance are secured before belt tension is released.
steps.failure_condition: The M6 socket-head screws cannot be installed below both carriages or the A-axis and counterbalance remain unsecured.

actions.label: Install retaining screws below carriages
actions.exact_action: Thread M6 socket-head screws into the holes below both carriages to prevent the A-axis and counterbalance from dropping once belt tension is released.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Idler pulley assembly figure and adjacent A-axis/counterbalance area near the motor access area where the M6 socket-head screws are installed below both carriages.

stop_or_escalate_if: The A-axis and counterbalance cannot be positioned and secured with M6 socket-head screws as described.
The M6 socket-head screws cannot be installed below both carriages.

source_evidence.text: Thread M6 socket-head screws into the holes below both carriages to prevent the A-axis and counterbalance from dropping once belt tension is released.

The removal steps instruct technicians to position the A-axis and counterbalance between threaded holes in the actuator base before inserting M6 socket-head screws to prevent dropping when belt tension is released.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Figure 7-21 appears on page 185 in section optisweep_operation_and_maintenance_manual_final_1_7_3_20_1_removal.

success_criteria: The motor access area is exposed if required.
The A-axis and counterbalance are positioned between the threaded holes in the actuator base.
The carriages are positioned between the adjacent holes on the respective rails.
M6 socket-head screws are installed below both carriages before belt tension is released.
healthy_conditions: The station side panel is removed if needed for motor access.
Two holes are identified adjacent to each rail.
The carriages are positioned between the holes on the rails.
The A-axis and counterbalance are secured against dropping.
failure_conditions: The station side panel cannot be removed when motor access is needed.
The A-axis and counterbalance cannot be positioned between the threaded holes in the actuator base.
The carriages are not positioned between the holes on the respective rails.
M6 socket-head screws cannot be installed below both carriages.
The A-axis and counterbalance are not secured before belt tension is released.
escalation_guidance: Stop and escalate if lockout/tagout cannot be completed.
Stop and escalate if the A-axis and counterbalance cannot be positioned and secured with M6 socket-head screws as described.
Escalate for additional source material because the provided source section is truncated and does not include the remaining removal or installation steps.
